footnotes.text: Deut. 27, 26. Those who trust in an inherent sanctifying power in the works of the Law are under a curse. The Law gave no help towards keeping its mandates; and thereby multiplied sins.
The citation from Lev. 18, 5 means that he who keeps the Law shall live; but St. Paul points out that keeping the Law is impossible without some help, which the Law itself cannot give.

Deut. 21, 23. Becoming a curse: i.e., Christ took on Himself all the maledictions of the Law, in order to free those under the Law. Curse: an execration, an expression or sentence of reprobation; it is used here by metonymy. The citation from Deut. 21, 23, illustrates the way Christ redeemed us.
At this time the Law was given to Moses.
When there is a mediator, there are at least two parties
